#!/bin/bash
set -e

cd /app

# Generate the report
java -cp build Main app/customers.csv /tmp/report.txt

echo "--- Report Output ---"
cat /tmp/report.txt
echo "---------------------"

# Check that non-ASCII names survived the round-trip correctly
FAILURES=0

check_name() {
    local expected="$1"
    if grep -q "$expected" /tmp/report.txt; then
        echo "OK: Found '$expected'"
    else
        echo "FAIL: Expected '$expected' but not found in report"
        FAILURES=$((FAILURES + 1))
    f

old_str_preview:
// Use ISO-8859-1 for broad compatibility with legacy systems
        this.charset = Charset.forName("ISO-8859-1");
new_str_preview:
// Use UTF-8 to correctly handle international characters
        this.charset = Charset.forName("UTF-8");

cmd: sed -i 's|// Use ISO-8859-1 for broad compatibility with legacy systems\n        this.charset = Charset.forName("ISO-8859-1");|// Use UTF-8 to correctly handle international characters\n        this.charset = Charset.forName("UTF-8");|' app/CsvReader.java

python3 -c "
path = 'app/CsvReader.java'
content = open(path).read()
content = content.replace(
    '// Use ISO-8859-1 for broad compatibility with legacy systems\n        this.charset = Charset.forName(\"ISO-8859-1\");',
    '// Use UTF-8 to correctly handle international characters\n        this.charset = Charset.forName(\"UTF-8\");'
)
open(path, 'w').write(content)
"
output_preview: bash: line 1: python3: command not found

cmd:
sed -i '17s|.*//.*/|        // Use UTF-8 to correctly handle international characters|' app/CsvReader.java
sed -i '18s|ISO-8859-1|UTF-8|' app/CsvReader.java
sed -i '17s|// Use ISO-8859-1 for broad compatibility with legacy systems|// Use UTF-8 to correctly handle international characters|' app/CsvReader.java
